heading: Facilities financing assistance
label: 7221c Facilities financing assistance

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: From the amount reserved under , the Secretary shall use not less than 50 percent to award, on a competitive basis, not less than 3 grants to eligible entities that have the highest-quality applications approved under subsection (d), after considering the diversity of such applications, to demonstrate innovative methods of helping charter schools to address the cost of acquiring, constructing, and renovating facilities by enhancing the availability of loans or bond financing.section 7221a(b)(1) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: a public entity, such as a State or local governmental entity;

children.label: B
children.text: a private nonprofit entity; or

children.label: C
children.text: a consortium of entities described in subparagraphs (A) and (B).

children.heading: Grantee selection
children.label: b Grantee selection
children.text: The Secretary shall evaluate each application submitted under subsection (d), and shall determine whether the application is sufficient to merit approval.

children.heading: Grant characteristics
children.label: c Grant characteristics
children.text: Grants under subsection (a) shall be of sufficient size, scope, and quality so as to ensure an effective demonstration of an innovative means of enhancing credit for the financing of charter school acquisition, construction, or renovation.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: An eligible entity desiring to receive a grant under this section shall submit an application to the Secretary in such form as the Secretary may reasonably require.

children.label: A
children.text: a statement identifying the activities that the eligible entity proposes to carry out with funds received under subsection (a), including how the eligible entity will determine which charter schools will receive assistance, and how much and what types of assistance charter schools will receive;

children.label: B
children.text: a description of the involvement of charter schools in the application’s development and the design of the proposed activities;

children.label: C
children.text: a description of the eligible entity’s expertise in capital market financing;

children.label: D
children.text: a description of how the proposed activities will leverage the maximum amount of private-sector financing capital relative to the amount of government funding used and otherwise enhance credit available to charter schools, including how the eligible entity will offer a combination of rates and terms more favorable than the rates and terms that a charter school could receive without assistance from the eligible entity under this section;

children.label: E
children.text: a description of how the eligible entity possesses sufficient expertise in education to evaluate the likelihood of success of a charter school program for which facilities financing is sought; and

children.label: F
children.text: in the case of an application submitted by a State governmental entity, a description of the actions that the eligible entity has taken, or will take, to ensure that charter schools within the State receive the funding that charter schools need to have adequate facilities.

The acquisition (by purchase, lease, donation, or otherwise) of an interest (including an interest held by a third party for the benefit of a charter school) in improved or unimproved real property that is necessary to commence or continue the operation of a charter school.
The construction of new facilities, or the renovation, repair, or alteration of existing facilities, necessary to commence or continue the operation of a charter school.
The predevelopment costs required to assess sites for purposes of paragraph (1) or (2) and that are necessary to commence or continue the operation of a charter school.

children.label: A
children.text: Guaranteeing, insuring, and reinsuring bonds, notes, evidences of debt, loans, and interests therein, the proceeds of which are used for an objective described in subsection (e).

children.label: B
children.text: Guaranteeing and insuring leases of personal and real property for an objective described in subsection (e).

children.label: C
children.text: Facilitating financing by identifying potential lending sources, encouraging private lending, and other similar activities that directly promote lending to, or for the benefit of, charter schools.

children.label: D
children.text: Facilitating the issuance of bonds by charter schools, or by other public entities for the benefit of charter schools, by providing technical, administrative, and other appropriate assistance (including the recruitment of bond counsel, underwriters, and potential investors and the consolidation of multiple charter school projects within a single bond issue).

children.heading: Investment
children.label: 2 Investment
children.text: Funds received under subsection (a) and deposited in the reserve account established under paragraph (1) shall be invested in obligations issued or guaranteed by the United States or a State, or in other similarly low-risk securities.

children.heading: Reinvestment of earnings
children.label: 3 Reinvestment of earnings
children.text: Any earnings on funds received under subsection (a) shall be deposited in the reserve account established under paragraph (1) and used in accordance with this subsection.

children.heading: Limitation on administrative costs
children.label: g Limitation on administrative costs
children.text: An eligible entity may use not more than 2.5 percent of the funds received under subsection (a) for the administrative costs of carrying out its responsibilities under this section (excluding subsection (k)).

children.heading: Financial record maintenance and audit
children.label: 1 Financial record maintenance and audit
children.text: The financial records of each eligible entity receiving a grant under subsection (a) shall be maintained in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles and shall be subject to an annual audit by an independent public accountant.

children.heading: Grantee annual reports
children.label: A Grantee annual reports
children.text: Each eligible entity receiving a grant under subsection (a) shall submit to the Secretary an annual report of the entity’s operations and activities under this section (excluding subsection (k)).

children.label: i
children.text: a copy of the most recent financial statements, and any accompanying opinion on such statements, prepared by the independent public accountant reviewing the financial records of the eligible entity;

children.label: ii
children.text: a copy of any report made on an audit of the financial records of the eligible entity that was conducted under paragraph (1) during the reporting period;

children.label: iii
children.text: an evaluation by the eligible entity of the effectiveness of its use of the Federal funds provided under subsection (a) in leveraging private funds;

children.label: iv
children.text: a listing and description of the charter schools served during the reporting period, including the amount of funds used by each school, the type of project facilitated by the grant, and the type of assistance provided to the charter schools;

children.label: v
children.text: a description of the activities carried out by the eligible entity to assist charter schools in meeting the objectives set forth in subsection (e); and

children.label: vi
children.text: a description of the characteristics of lenders and other financial institutions participating in the activities carried out by the eligible entity under this section (excluding subsection (k)) during the reporting period.

children.heading: Secretarial report
children.label: C Secretarial report
children.text: The Secretary shall review the reports submitted under subparagraph (A) and shall provide a comprehensive annual report to Congress on the activities conducted under this section (excluding subsection (k)).

children.heading: No full faith and credit for grantee obligation
children.label: i No full faith and credit for grantee obligation
children.text: No financial obligation of an eligible entity entered into pursuant to this section (such as an obligation under a guarantee, bond, note, evidence of debt, or loan) shall be an obligation of, or guaranteed in any respect by, the United States. The full faith and credit of the United States is not pledged to the payment of funds that may be required to be paid under any obligation made by an eligible entity pursuant to any provision of this section.

children.label: A
children.text: all of the funds in a reserve account established by an eligible entity under subsection (f)(1) if the Secretary determines, not earlier than 2 years after the date on which the eligible entity first received funds under subsection (a), that the eligible entity has failed to make substantial progress in carrying out the purposes described in subsection (f)(1); or

children.label: B
children.text: all or a portion of the funds in a reserve account established by an eligible entity under subsection (f)(1) if the Secretary determines that the eligible entity has permanently ceased to use all or a portion of the funds in such account to accomplish any purpose described in subsection (f)(1).

children.heading: Exercise of authority
children.label: 2 Exercise of authority
children.text: The Secretary shall not exercise the authority provided in paragraph (1) to collect from any eligible entity any funds that are being properly used to achieve one or more of the purposes described in subsection (f)(1).

children.heading: Procedures
children.label: 3 Procedures
children.text: The provisions of sections 451, 452, and 458 of the General Education Provisions Act [, 1234a, 1234g] shall apply to the recovery of funds under paragraph (1).20 U.S.C. 1234

children.heading: Construction
children.label: 4 Construction
children.text: This subsection shall not be construed to impair or affect the authority of the Secretary to recover funds under part D of the General Education Provisions Act ( et seq.).20 U.S.C. 1234

children.heading: Definition of per-pupil facilities aid program
children.label: 1 Definition of per-pupil facilities aid program
A
children.text: that is dedicated solely to funding charter school facilities; or

children.label: B
children.text: a portion of which is dedicated for funding charter school facilities.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: From the amount reserved under and remaining after the Secretary makes grants under subsection (a), the Secretary shall make grants, on a competitive basis, to States to pay for the Federal share of the cost of establishing or enhancing, and administering, per-pupil facilities aid programs.section 7221a(b)(1) of this title

children.heading: Period
children.label: B Period
children.text: The Secretary shall award grants under this subsection for periods of not more than 5 years.

children.label: i
children.text: 90 percent of the cost, for the first fiscal year for which the program receives assistance under this subsection;

children.label: ii
children.text: 80 percent for the second such year;

children.label: iii
children.text: 60 percent for the third such year;

children.label: iv
children.text: 40 percent for the fourth such year; and

children.label: v
children.text: 20 percent for the fifth such year.

children.heading: State share
children.label: D State share
children.text: A State receiving a grant under this subsection may partner with 1 or more organizations, and such organizations may provide not more than 50 percent of the State share of the cost of establishing or enhancing, and administering, the per-pupil facilities aid program.

children.heading: Multiple grants
children.label: E Multiple grants
children.text: A State may receive more than 1 grant under this subsection, so long as the amount of total funds provided to charter schools increases with each successive grant.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A State that receives a grant under this subsection shall use the funds made available through the grant to establish or enhance, and administer, a per-pupil facilities aid program for charter schools in the State of the applicant.

children.heading: Evaluations; technical assistance; dissemination
children.label: B Evaluations; technical assistance; dissemination
children.text: From the amount made available to a State through a grant under this subsection for a fiscal year, the State may reserve not more than 5 percent to carry out evaluations, to provide technical assistance, and to disseminate information.

children.heading: Supplement, not supplant
children.label: C Supplement, not supplant
children.text: Funds made available under this subsection shall be used to supplement, and not supplant, State and local public funds expended to provide per-pupil facilities aid programs, operations financing programs, or other programs, for charter schools.

children.heading: Voluntary participation
children.label: A Voluntary participation
children.text: No State may be required to participate in a program carried out under this subsection.

children.label: I
children.text: is specified in State law; and

children.label: II
children.text: provides annual financing, on a per-pupil basis, for charter school facilities.

children.heading: Special rule
children.label: ii Special rule
children.text: A State that is required under State law to provide its charter schools with access to adequate facility space, but that does not have a per-pupil facilities aid program for charter schools specified in State law, is eligible to receive a grant under this subsection if the State agrees to use the funds to develop a per-pupil facilities aid program consistent with the requirements of this subsection.

children.heading: Applications
children.label: 5 Applications
children.text: To be eligible to receive a grant under this subsection, a State shall submit an application to the Secretary at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the Secretary may require.
